rank_rule: Rank 1 is the highest observed value for the selected index. Percentiles run from 0 to 100, with 100 assigned to the highest observed value.

indices.label: Export Competition Index (ECI)
indices.description: Model-based exposure of a country's exports to increased Chinese competition in third markets. It combines the product-level China price shock, Chinese penetration in destination-product markets, the importance of those markets for the exporter, and demand elasticities.

indices.label: Export Competition Index, product-level variant (ECI_prod)
indices.description: Product-level variant of the export competition index that aggregates across destinations first. It is useful when Chinese exports may be redirected toward destinations where China had relatively low baseline sales.

indices.label: Export Competition Index, long-window variant (ECI_long)
indices.description: Export competition index using the longer tariff window and earlier baseline shares.

indices.label: Export Competition Index, basic variant (ECI_basic)
indices.description: Export competition index using a simpler price-shock proxy rather than the full elasticity- and tariff-based shock.

indices.label: Export Competition Index, basic full-coverage variant (ECI_basic_full)
indices.description: Basic export competition index extended to a broader product set where possible.

indices.label: Export Similarity Index (ESI)
indices.description: Finger-Kreinin-style similarity between a country's export structure and China's across products and destinations outside the United States and China.

indices.label: Export Similarity Index, destination-weighted variant (ESI_alt)
indices.description: Alternative export similarity measure that averages destination-specific product similarity using the country's export weights across destinations.

indices.label: Export Similarity Index, product-only variant (ESI_prod)
indices.description: Export similarity computed at the product level, abstracting from the destination composition of sales.

indices.label: Export Similarity Index, destination-only variant (ESI_dest)
indices.description: Export similarity computed at the destination level, abstracting from the product composition of sales.

indices.label: Import Competition Index (ICI)
indices.description: Model-based exposure of domestic producers to redirected Chinese goods in their own market. It weights product-level import pressure by the importance of the affected sectors for domestic sales.

indices.label: Import Competition Index, long-window variant (ICI_long)
indices.description: Import competition index using the longer tariff window and earlier baseline shares.

indices.label: Import Competition Index, basic variant (ICI_basic)
indices.description: Import competition index using a simpler price-shock proxy rather than the full elasticity- and tariff-based shock.

indices.label: Import Competition Index, basic full-coverage variant (ICI_basic_full)
indices.description: Basic import competition index extended to a broader product set where possible.

indices.label: Import Similarity Index (ISI)
indices.description: Similarity between the products a country imports from China and the products the United States imports from China.

indices.label: Import Similarity Index, product variant (ISI_prod)
indices.description: Alternative import similarity measure focused on product composition.

indices.label: Import Similarity Index, broad variant (ISI_broad)
indices.description: Broad import similarity measure comparing a country's imports from China with China's exports to all destinations, not only the United States.

indices.label: Consumer Gains Index (CGI)
indices.description: Exposure of consumers to lower import prices from redirected Chinese goods. Higher values indicate larger potential price declines for import consumers.

indices.label: Consumer Gains Index, long-window variant (CGI_long)
indices.description: Consumer gains index using the longer tariff window and earlier baseline shares.

indices.label: Consumer Gains Index, basic variant (CGI_basic)
indices.description: Consumer gains index using a simpler price-shock proxy rather than the full elasticity- and tariff-based shock.

indices.label: Consumer Gains Index, basic full-coverage variant (CGI_basic_full)
indices.description: Basic consumer gains index extended to a broader product set where possible.

indices.label: Consumer Gains Index, upstream goods (CGI_upstream)
indices.description: Consumer gains index restricted to more upstream goods, using the upstreamness classification.

indices.label: Consumer Gains Index, downstream goods (CGI_downstream)
indices.description: Consumer gains index restricted to more downstream or final goods, using the upstreamness classification.

indices.label: Sourcing Gains Index (SGI)
indices.description: Exposure of downstream producers to cheaper Chinese intermediate inputs. The index traces input-output links from cheaper imported inputs to downstream export competitiveness.

indices.label: Sourcing Gains Index, long-window variant (SGI_long)
indices.description: Sourcing gains index using the longer tariff window and earlier baseline shares.

indices.label: Sourcing Gains Index, basic variant (SGI_basic)
indices.description: Sourcing gains index using a simpler price-shock proxy rather than the full elasticity- and tariff-based shock.

indices.label: Sourcing Gains Index, basic full-coverage variant (SGI_basic_full)
indices.description: Basic sourcing gains index extended to a broader product set where possible.
